questions.ai_explanation: Manasseh's shedding of innocent blood was among his great sins that provoked God to send bands of Chaldeans, Syrians, Moabites and Ammonites against Judah (2 Kings 24:3-4).

questions.subject: crk
questions.question: Elijah decreed that there would be neither rain nor dew for three years because

options.A: Ahab had forsaken the commandment of God by erecting an altar for Baal
options.B: Nebat the son of Rehoboam connived with Ahab to kill him
options.C: he was sure that he would be fed by the ravens
options.D: Jezebel the wife of Ahab killed Obadiah.

questions.ai_explanation: Elijah declared a drought because Ahab had forsaken God's commandments and erected an altar for Baal, turning to worship false gods (1 Kings 17:1 and context).

questions.subject: crk
questions.question: God called Jonah and send him to the people of Nineveh to

options.B: pray to Him for their forgiveness

questions.ai_explanation: God sent Jonah to Nineveh to cry against the city's wickedness, calling them to repentance (Jonah 1:2).

questions.subject: crk
questions.question: Consequent upon the murder of Naboth by Ahab and Jezebel, God declared that

options.A: dogs would lick Ahab’s blood where they had licked Naboth’s
options.B: Ahab’s descendants would never ascend the throne is Israel
options.C: He would require the blood of Naboth from Ahab’s hand
options.D: the sword would not depart from the house of Ahab.

questions.ai_explanation: God declared that dogs would lick Ahab's blood in the same place where they licked Naboth's blood as a judgment for the murder and theft of Naboth's vineyard (1 Kings 21:19).

questions.subject: crk
questions.question: According to Amos, God decided to raise up a nation that would fight and oppress Israel because the people

options.A: were corrupt and enslaved the poor
options.B: claimed to achieve fame by their own strength
options.C: rejected him and sold the righteous for silver

questions.ai_explanation: According to Amos, God would raise a nation against Israel because they were corrupt, oppressed the poor, and enslaved them (Amos 6:11-14).

questions.subject: crk
questions.question: What did king Josiah do to the altar that was erected by Jeroboam at Bethel?

options.C: He broke its stones into pieces

questions.ai_explanation: King Josiah broke the altar that Jeroboam erected at Bethel into pieces and burned the Asherah pole as part of his religious reforms (2 Kings 23:15).

questions.subject: crk
questions.question: ‘ “ Before I formed you in the womb I knew you, and before you were born I consecrated you; I appointed you a prophet to the nations.’’ ’ The statements above was made by God during the

options.B: he was very excited about the discovery
options.C: he realized that God was angry with Israel
options.D: no one was able to read the word of the book.

questions.ai_explanation: This quote is from God's call to the prophet Jeremiah, not Isaiah, during his consecration as a prophet to the nations (Jeremiah 1:5).

questions.subject: crk
questions.question: According to Jeremiah, other nations would find glory in God and bless themselves in Him only when Israel

options.A: swears in truth, in justice and in uprightness
options.B: becomes loyal to God and repents of her sins
options.C: returns to God, honours and glorifies Him
options.D: comes to God with prayer and fasting

questions.ai_explanation: According to Jeremiah, other nations would find glory in God and bless themselves in Him when Israel swears in truth, in justice and in uprightness (Jeremiah 4:2).

questions.subject: crk
questions.question: Hosea proclaimed that Israel should return to God and plead that he should
questions.ai_explanation: Hosea proclaimed that Israel should return to God and plead with Him to take away their iniquity and accept them (Hosea 14:2).

questions.subject: crk
questions.question: The response of Eli after Samuel had told him the Lord’s message as regards his sons’ acts of blasphemy was that

options.A: sacrifice would be offered on their behalf
options.B: he would call them and rebuke them
options.C: God should have mercy on them
options.D: He was the LORD, and should do what pleased Him.

questions.ai_explanation: Eli's response to Samuel's message about his sons' blasphemy was acceptance that the LORD would do what pleased Him, demonstrating his submission to God's will (1 Samuel 3:18).

questions.subject: crk
questions.question: Rehab’s reward for hiding the messengers sent by Joshua to spy out the city of Jericho was that

options.A: she and her household were spared
options.B: the city of Jericho was spared for her sake
options.C: she married one of the spies and became a queen
options.D: the gold, bronze and iron in the city were given to her

questions.ai_explanation: Rahab and her entire household were spared from destruction when Jericho fell because of her assistance to Joshua's spies (Joshua 6:22-25).

questions.subject: crk
questions.question: David’s victory over he Amalekites who raided Ziklag was due to

options.A: the fact that David was a mighty man in battle
options.B: his swearing to the Egyptian servant of the Amalekites not to kill or harm him
options.C: the great army that followed him to fight the Amalekites
options.D: his prayer in seeking the approval of God before pursuing them

questions.ai_explanation: David's victory over the Amalekites was secured after he inquired of the LORD and received His approval to pursue them (1 Samuel 30:8).

questions.subject: crk
questions.question: God did not lead the Israelites by the way of the land of the Philistines although that was near because

options.A: He did not want them to see war and return to Egypt
options.B: The Egyptians would have overtaken them
options.C: They were afraid of the Philistines
options.D: He wanted them to learn through hardship in the wilderness

questions.ai_explanation: God did not lead Israel through the Philistines' land because He knew they would encounter war and desire to return to Egypt due to fear (Exodus 13:17).

questions.subject: crk
questions.question: One of king Solomon’s unwise policies was the

options.A: incessant fighting of wars against his enemies
options.B: signing of treaties with the kings of the surrounding nations
options.C: use of forced labour to build the house of God
options.D: making of sacrifices in high places. [PAGE 59]

questions.ai_explanation: Solomon's use of forced labour to build the house of God and other structures was considered unwise and eventually led to rebellion under his son Rehoboam (1 Kings 12:4).

questions.subject: crk
questions.question: ‘ “It is not good that the man should be alone; I will make him a helper fit for him.’’ ’ After this statement, the first thing God did was to

options.A: form beasts and bird and bring them to the man
options.B: take one of the man’s ribs and close up its place with flesh
options.C: make a rib into a woman and bring her to the man
options.D: cause a deep sleep to fall upon the man.

questions.ai_explanation: After God said He would make a helper for man, the first action was to cause a deep sleep to fall upon Adam before creating woman from his rib (Genesis 2:21).

questions.subject: crk
questions.question: Joseph’s brothers decided not to kill him because

options.C: was their brother and their flesh
options.D: was the first son of his mother

questions.ai_explanation: Joseph's brothers decided not to kill him because he was their brother and their flesh, with Reuben specifically appealing to this kinship (Genesis 37:26-27).

questions.subject: crk
questions.question: King Saul ordered that those who had opposed his ascent to the throne should not be put to death because

options.A: God had wrought deliverance in Israel
options.B: They had fought bravely in battle
options.C: They had been reconciled with him
options.D: God had forgiven their evil intentions

questions.ai_explanation: Saul ordered that those who opposed him not be put to death because God had wrought deliverance in Israel, recognizing God's hand in his ascension (1 Samuel 11:13).

questions.subject: crk
questions.question: After Saul realized that God would have nothing to do with him, he sought the help of
questions.ai_explanation: After realizing God had abandoned him, Saul sought help from a medium (the witch at Endor) to contact Samuel's spirit (1 Samuel 28:7-8).

questions.subject: crk
questions.question: The LORD said to Moses that the people should gather just a day’s portion of bread so that He might test their
questions.ai_explanation: The LORD told Moses the people should gather only a day's portion of manna so He might test their obedience to His instructions (Exodus 16:4).

questions.subject: crk
questions.question: What were the Israelites asked to do before meeting with God on Mount Sinai?

options.A: To be consecrated have their garments washed
options.B: To touch the border of the mountain
options.C: To fast and have their feet washed
options.D: To fast, pray and be consecrated.

questions.ai_explanation: Before meeting God on Mount Sinai, the Israelites were commanded to be consecrated and have their garments washed (Exodus 19:10).

questions.subject: crk
questions.question: ‘ “ To you I will give all this authority and their glory; for it has been delivered to me, and I give it to whom I will…”’ This statement was made during the
questions.ai_explanation: This statement about being given authority and glory was made by Satan during the temptation of Jesus in the wilderness (Luke 4:6).

questions.subject: crk
questions.question: ‘ “ … Just so, I tell you, there is joy before the angels of God over on sinner who repents.’’ ’ Jesus made this statements after telling the parable of the
questions.ai_explanation: Jesus made this statement about joy over a repenting sinner after telling the parable of the lost coin (Luke 15:8-10).

questions.subject: crk
questions.question: According to Luke, the leper healed by Jesus was commanded to

options.A: go and show himself to his friends
options.B: find out from the priest what he should offer
options.D: make an offering for his cleansing.

questions.ai_explanation: Jesus commanded the healed leper to go to the priest and make an offering for his cleansing as required by the law (Luke 5:14).

questions.subject: crk
questions.question: After the great confession by Peter, Jesus charged the disciple to

options.A: announce that the kingdom of heaven had c ome
options.B: tell no one that he was the Christ
options.C: go away from the crowd to rest
options.D: beware of the leaven of the Pharisees.

questions.ai_explanation: After Peter's great confession that Jesus is the Christ, Jesus charged the disciples to tell no one that he was the Christ (Matthew 16:20).

questions.subject: crk
questions.question: The Angel of the Lord told Zachariah that the son would

options.A: make the disobedient repent of their sins
options.B: turn may of the sons and daughters of Israel to God
options.C: go before God in the spirit and power of Elijah
options.D: turn the hearts of the children to their father.

questions.ai_explanation: The Angel Gabriel told Zachariah that John the Baptist would go before God in the spirit and power of Elijah (Luke 1:17).

questions.subject: crk
questions.question: When they returned from their mission, the seventy reported that

options.C: they saw Satan fall like lightning
options.D: even demons were subject to them

questions.ai_explanation: The seventy disciples reported to Jesus that even demons were subject to them in his name (Luke 10:17).

questions.subject: crk
questions.question: “Take heart, it is I; have no fear.’’’ This statement was made by Jesus when

options.A: He appeared to two disciples on the way to Emmaus
options.B: He was walking on the sea in the night
options.C: Mary Magdalene saw him at the sepulehre
options.D: The women saw Him after resurrection

questions.ai_explanation: Jesus said 'Take heart, it is I; have no fear' when he was walking on the sea in the night (Matthew 14:27).

questions.subject: crk
questions.question: “… Blessed is the kingdom of our father David that is coming…” The declaration above was made by the crowd during the
questions.ai_explanation: The declaration 'Blessed is the kingdom of our father David that is coming' was made by the crowd during Jesus's triumphal entry into Jerusalem (Mark 11:10).

questions.subject: crk
questions.question: After healing the man at the pool of Bathzatha, the Jews sought all the more to kill Jesus because

options.A: they hated the man that was healed
options.B: he did his on the Sabbath
options.C: they were unhappy that he healed the man
options.D: he claimed that God his father was still working

questions.ai_explanation: The Jews sought to kill Jesus because he healed the man at the pool of Bethzatha on the Sabbath, violating their Sabbath laws (John 5:16).
